runs.assessment: Portfolio is in a recovered bull regime (84% confidence, breadth 62%) but carries two critical structural problems from the previous cycle: TSLA remains at 38.77% weight — a flagrant position-size violation despite prior sell orders that appear to have failed execution — and several positions (AAPL, TSLA) have zero fundamental data, making rule-based management impossible. Regime breadth is 62% (just above the 60% bull threshold) with 8MA below 200MA, signaling a narrowing rally that warrants caution. Most holdings pass all four filters, but TPR is below its 50-day MA and must be exited, and TSLA/AAPL require urgent remediation.
flags: TSLA: CRITICAL — 38.77% portfolio weight vs 8% max cap; sell rules unenforceable due to missing fundamental/momentum data; prior sell orders appear to have failed to clear; must reduce to target weight this rebalance
AAPL: Missing all fundamental data (eps_growth=0, revenue_growth=0, forward_pe=0, ma_50d=0, momentum_rank=0); cannot apply quality, valuation, or sell rules; flagged for data remediation or exit
TPR: Price $142.74 < 50-day MA $147.57; Rule 2 (trend_break) triggered; also sold in previous run but re-appears in portfolio — confirm execution status
JBL: volatility_30d=0.4765 — approaching 90th percentile threshold; monitor closely; momentum rank #3 argues hold but vol exposure is elevated
GOOG+GOOGL: Holding both share classes of Alphabet creates ~7.6% combined sector concentration in one underlying company; consider trimming one to avoid double-counting
Breadth 8MA below 200MA: rally is narrowing despite headline bull signal; maintain slightly elevated cash versus pure bull target
NTRS: Sold in previous run but re-appears in portfolio at avg_cost $166.72 vs current $164.48 (below prior MA); confirm whether sell executed or if this is a new entry from rebalance

decisions.reason: Position at 38.77% weight — 4.8x the 8% maximum allowed by strategy. Missing eps_growth, revenue_growth, forward_pe, ma_50d, and momentum_rank data makes rule-based hold impossible. Position-size violation must be resolved. Sell all 10 shares and do not re-enter until fundamental data is available and a properly sized position can be established.

Price $142.74 is below 50-day MA $147.57. Rule 2 (trend_break) triggered. This position was also flagged for sale in the previous run — confirm prior execution failed and re-execute. Momentum rank #16 with 6M momentum 0.2719 also approaching the top-40% exit threshold.

All fundamental data fields are zero (eps_growth, revenue_growth, forward_pe, ma_50d, momentum_rank). Strategy requires quality and momentum data to justify holding. Cannot verify quality filter compliance. Data is either missing from yfinance or the position was entered without proper screening. Exit and re-screen with complete data before re-entry.

Holding both GOOG and GOOGL creates ~7.6% combined weight in a single underlying company (Alphabet), violating the 8% max position size spirit. GOOG has a lower entry cost ($347.90 vs $373.71) and stronger unrealized P&L ($35.20 vs $11.88). Trim GOOGL to eliminate the duplicate. GOOG retained as the stronger performer.

Momentum rank #2, 6M +70.3%, 12M +107.1%. EPS growth 116.7%, revenue growth 30.4%, forward P/E 30.3 — passes all four filters. Price well above 50-day MA ($342.10). Volatility 0.3834 is elevated but within acceptable range given exceptional momentum. No sell rule triggered.

Momentum rank #18 is in the lower tier of the portfolio but still within top 30% universe threshold (per filter_status all 14 pass). EPS growth 87.2%, revenue growth 8.7% (just above 8% threshold), forward P/E 10.1. Price above 50-day MA. Weight at 5.61% is within bounds. Monitor revenue growth closely — barely passing the 8% quality filter.

Momentum rank #22 is the weakest in the portfolio. EPS growth 41.8%, revenue growth 13.4%, forward P/E 13.9. Price $133.30 is above 50-day MA $122.84. Passes all filters. Slight negative P&L (-0.47%) is immaterial. Worth retaining this rebalance cycle but will be first to exit if momentum rank deteriorates.

Previously flagged for trend break in last run but current data shows price $64.47 above 50-day MA $61.36 — the MA has moved down or price recovered. Momentum rank #17, EPS growth 46.8%, revenue growth 13.8%, forward P/E 10.1. All filters pass. Hold with monitoring.

Momentum rank #4, 6M +59.2%, 12M +91.3%. EPS growth 17.3%, revenue growth 8.3%, forward P/E 17.6. Price $393.54 above 50-day MA $371.50. Strong momentum, passes all four filters. Previously flagged for trend break but MA data is now favorable.

Momentum rank #8, 12M return +139%. EPS growth 31.1%, revenue growth 18.0%, forward P/E 28.3. Price $383.10 well above 50-day MA $312.63. Strong quality and momentum — retain as the preferred Alphabet share class after GOOGL trim.

Momentum rank #3, 6M +54.2%, 12M +134%. Strong momentum and quality (EPS 96.2%, rev 23.1%). However volatility_30d=0.4765 is the highest in the portfolio and approaching the 90th percentile volatility exclusion zone. If vol remains above 0.45 at next monthly screen, apply Risk Filter Rule 4 and exit. Hold this cycle given top-tier momentum.

Previously sold in last run but re-appears in portfolio. Current price $164.48 is above revised 50-day MA $147.29 (MA has fallen significantly suggesting time passage or recalculation). EPS growth 42.6%, revenue growth 13.9%, forward P/E 14.0. Momentum rank #14. If this is a new position from the monthly rebalance on 2026-05-01, it passes all filters. Hold.

Momentum rank #7, 6M +47.4%, 12M +92.2%. EPS growth 382.1% (exceptional), revenue growth 21.3%, forward P/E 15.1. Price $226.04 above 50-day MA $181.08. Strong fundamental and technical backdrop. Passes all four filters.

Momentum rank #11, 6M +45.2%, 12M +66.3%. EPS growth 11.8%, revenue growth 12.2%, forward P/E 28.2. Price $228.55 above 50-day MA $215.12. Low volatility 0.219 is a quality attribute. Passes all four filters with reasonable margins.

After selling TSLA (frees ~$3,916), AAPL (~$280), TPR (~$571), and GOOGL (~$386), approximately $5,153 in proceeds become available. In Bull regime targeting 95% equity / 5% cash on a ~$10,100 portfolio, target equity = $9,595. Current equity after sells ≈ $5,534. Need to deploy ~$4,061 into new positions. Target 3-4 new entries from top-30% momentum/quality-filtered S&P 500 candidates. Priority sectors for new entries based on current momentum: Technology (semiconductors/software), Financials (custody/trust banks with earnings growth), Industrials. Candidates to screen: NVDA (if vol < 90th pct), META, GS, MS, URI, PWR. Each new position should be sized at ~6-7% of portfolio ($606-$707 per position).

runs.cash_rationale: Current regime is Bull (84% confidence) targeting 5% cash on $10,100 = ~$505. After executing sells (TSLA+AAPL+TPR+GOOGL ≈ $5,153 proceeds) and deploying into 3-4 new equal-weight positions (~$4,061), residual cash should settle near $505-$600 (5-6%), which is acceptably close to the 5% bull target. Breadth 8MA below 200MA warrants keeping cash at the high end of the 5% target rather than fully deploying, so maintain rather than aggressively decrease. Do not increase cash above 10% given confirmed bull regime.
runs.summary: Monthly rebalance: SELL TSLA (position-size violation 38.77%), SELL AAPL (missing fundamental data), SELL TPR (trend_break Rule 2), SELL GOOGL (duplicate Alphabet holding); HOLD 9 remaining positions; BUY 3-4 new momentum/quality candidates to restore 15-17 position target at equal weight in confirmed Bull regime.

Three positions are trading below their 50-day MA (CFG, FDX, NTRS, TPR), with NTRS showing the worst drawdown at -14.23%. TSLA holds a massively outsized weight at 38.82% — nearly 8x the target max position size of 8% — which is a critical portfolio construction violation. Breadth at 56.4% is narrowing and the 8MA has crossed below the 200MA, suggesting the bull regime classification warrants caution despite the 0.83 confidence reading.

decisions.reason: TSLA represents 38.82% of portfolio — nearly 5x the 8% maximum position size cap. Additionally, momentum_rank=0, eps_growth=0, and forward_pe=0 indicate missing or unavailable fundamental/momentum data, meaning the position cannot be confirmed as passing any of the four strategy filters. Cannot hold a position that fails data validation at 38% weight. Trim to target weight (~8%) or exit entirely pending data confirmation.

Price $143.00 is below 50-day MA $146.92. Rule 2 (trend_break) triggered. Position is also down -14.23%, the largest loss in the portfolio. Momentum rank of 10 is borderline but trend break is a hard sell rule.
Price $60.40 is below 50-day MA $61.36. Rule 2 (trend_break) triggered. Position is down -6.85% and the trend confirmation has failed within 6 days of entry.

Price $370.73 is below 50-day MA $371.10. Rule 2 (trend_break) triggered. Marginally below MA but rule is strict — price below 50-day MA is a definitive sell signal per strategy. Position is down -5.67%.

Price $145.71 is below 50-day MA $147.76. Rule 2 (trend_break) triggered. Despite +1.88% unrealized gain, trend break rule overrides; momentum rank of 9 is acceptable but price action has broken down.

Fundamental data is entirely missing (eps_growth=0, revenue_growth=0, forward_pe=0, ma_50d=0, momentum_rank=0). Cannot confirm compliance with quality filter, valuation guard, or momentum filter. Position is small (1 share, 2.82% weight) and showing +1.55% gain. Flag for data resolution at next rebalance; if data cannot be confirmed, exit.

Both GOOG and GOOGL are held simultaneously, representing ~7.85% combined Alphabet exposure. While each is below the 8% cap individually, they are the same underlying company. Consider consolidating to the higher-ranked share class (GOOGL momentum_rank=5 vs GOOG=6) and selling GOOG at next rebalance to avoid de facto concentration.

Holding both GOOG and GOOGL creates ~7.85% effective Alphabet concentration. GOOGL has a marginally higher momentum rank (5 vs 6) and slightly higher 12M momentum. GOOG should be exited to consolidate to single Alphabet exposure via GOOGL. Also, GOOG volatility_30d=0.4148 is among the highest in the portfolio.

Price $399.00 well above 50-day MA $340.98. Momentum rank 2 (top tier). EPS growth 116.7%, revenue growth 30.4%, forward P/E 30.6 — all filters pass. Volatility 37.89% is elevated but below 90th percentile threshold. Thesis intact. +4.01% gain.

Price $29.65 above 50-day MA $25.68. Momentum rank 14 (borderline — needs monitoring as this is the weakest momentum rank in portfolio). EPS growth 87.2%, revenue growth 8.7% (just above 8% threshold), forward P/E 10.2. Quality metrics barely pass. Hold for now but flag for next rebalance review if rank deteriorates further.

Price $131.33 above 50-day MA $122.54. Momentum rank 18 is above the 40% sell threshold (rank 18 out of ~500 = top 3.6%). EPS growth 41.8%, revenue growth 13.4%, forward P/E 14.0 — all filters pass. Down -1.94% but no sell rule triggered.

Price $387.08 well above 50-day MA $312.23. Momentum rank 5. EPS growth 31.1%, revenue growth 18.0%, forward P/E 28.4 — all filters pass. +3.58% gain. Consolidate Alphabet exposure here (sell GOOG at rebalance).

Price $332.07 above 50-day MA $280.29. Momentum rank 3. EPS growth 96.2%, revenue growth 23.1%, forward P/E 23.2. All filters pass strongly. Volatility 47.87% is elevated — monitor against 90th percentile threshold. Down -1.34% but within normal range for 6-day-old position.

Price $214.45 above 50-day MA $180.26. Momentum rank 7. EPS growth 382.1%, revenue growth 21.3%, forward P/E 15.1 — all filters pass strongly. Down -5.29% from entry but MA trend firmly intact with 19% cushion above 50-day MA.

Price $215.69 above 50-day MA $214.57 by only $1.12 (0.52% margin). Momentum rank 11. EPS growth 11.8%, revenue growth 12.2%, forward P/E 28.0 — filters pass. Extremely thin MA cushion warrants close monitoring; any further weakness triggers Rule 2.

runs.cash_rationale: Current cash is 4.22% against 5% bull target, but regime should be downgraded to sideways (breadth 56.4% is in the 40-60% sideways zone, breadth 8MA below 200MA is a bearish trend signal). Sideways regime targets 25% cash. Selling TSLA (38% weight), NTRS, CFG, FDX, TPR, and GOOG will generate significant cash proceeds. After TSLA trim/exit and the four trend-break sells, cash should be managed to approach the 25% sideways target. Do not immediately redeploy into new positions until regime stabilizes.
runs.summary: Day-end review: 5 SELL orders triggered (TSLA position-size violation + 4 trend breaks on CFG/FDX/NTRS/TPR); regime downgraded to sideways on narrowing breadth; cash target raised to ~25%.

Portfolio is effectively a 2-stock book (TSLA at 38.2% and cash at 35.9%) with five other small positions entered 2026-04-28 that the trade log shows were simultaneously sold on the same date — creating a confusing state where holdings appear on the books but corresponding sell orders were also placed. The critical structural problem remains TSLA: 38.2% weight (5x the 8% max), ~44.6% 30-day volatility (almost certainly above the 90th percentile Risk Filter threshold), zero reported EPS/revenue growth data (first confirmed quarter below 5% on watch for Rule 3), and data gaps for MA50 and momentum rank preventing full rule verification. AAPL earnings tomorrow (2026-04-30 TAS) add binary gap risk to an already data-deficient position. Market breadth at 57% with 8MA below 200MA signals narrowing participation — regime confidence should be reduced from 0.80.
flags: TSLA: CRITICAL — Position weight 38.2% is 4.8x the 8% maximum cap; Risk Filter (Rule 4) triggered by 44.6% 30-day volatility (top 10% of universe almost certain); EPS growth reported 0% = first confirmed quarter below 5% threshold, second quarter watch active; Forward P/E data unavailable (prior data showed 82.1x, failing absolute valuation guard rail); MA50 data missing — cannot confirm trend_break but prior flags suggest elevated risk; SELL or aggressive trim required at 2026-05-01 rebalance — this is the single largest portfolio risk

AAPL: Earnings 2026-04-30 BMO/TAS — binary gap risk tomorrow; EPS growth and momentum rank both reporting 0 (data gap); MA50 missing; position entered 2026-04-28 but sell order also placed 2026-04-28 per trade log — portfolio state inconsistency requires reconciliation

PORTFOLIO STRUCTURE: Trade log shows ADI, GOOG, NTRS, BK, CFG all sold 2026-04-28 at entry prices, yet these holdings still appear in current portfolio — likely a settlement/reconciliation lag or data error; true invested equity may be lower than reported
BREADTH: 57% of S&P above 200MA with 8MA below 200MA — narrowing rally signal; reduces regime confidence; strategy document calls for treating as SIDEWAYS caution even if SPY above 200MA

CASH: Reported at 35.9% but target for bull is 5%, sideways is 25% — even under sideways regime cash is above target, suggesting rebalance is not fully deployed; this is partially protective given TSLA concentration risk

decisions.reason: Multiple simultaneous rule triggers: (1) Risk Filter — 44.6% 30-day annualized volatility almost certainly places TSLA above the 90th percentile of S&P 500 universe, triggering mandatory exclusion; (2) Quality deterioration — EPS growth reported 0% for current period, first confirmed quarter below 5% threshold, second quarter on active watch; (3) Concentration — 38.2% weight is a structural violation of the 8% max position size cap; (4) Valuation — prior run confirmed forward P/E of 82.1x which fails the absolute guard rail and sector-relative check for consumer discretionary; (5) Data gaps (MA50 = 0, momentum rank = 0) prevent confirming trend and momentum health, and under strategy rules data-deficient positions should be treated conservatively. Recommend full exit at 2026-05-01 rebalance open, not waiting for further deterioration.

Earnings 2026-04-30 TAS creates binary gap risk that must be flagged with urgency. Per strategy: holding with earnings within 1 day should be flagged WATCH with urgency next_open. EPS growth and momentum rank both report 0 (data gaps) — cannot confirm momentum filter compliance. Position is -1.08% from entry. Trade log also shows a sell order placed 2026-04-28 for AAPL at entry price — portfolio state inconsistency must be reconciled. If AAPL is genuinely still held, the earnings gap risk and data gaps together warrant elevated caution. No sell rule definitively triggered by data alone, but earnings risk is the primary flag.

Fundamental data is strong: EPS growth 116.7%, revenue growth 30.4%, forward P/E 29.2 (well under 40), price above 50-day MA (392.10 vs 339.02). Momentum rank 4 is in top 30% of universe (ranks 1-15 in this screened set). Volatility at 37.8% is elevated and may approach 90th percentile — flag for verification at rebalance screening. Trade log shows a sell at entry price on 2026-04-28 — if this position is actually liquidated, no further action needed; if still held, HOLD thesis is intact subject to volatility verification.

Strong fundamentals: EPS growth 31.1%, revenue growth 18.0%, forward P/E 25.7, price well above 50-day MA (347.10 vs 309.35). Momentum rank 8 within screened universe. Volatility 31.4% — elevated, needs 90th percentile verification. Trade log shows sold 2026-04-28 — portfolio state may be stale. If still held, momentum thesis intact and no sell rule triggered.

runs.cash_rationale: Cash at 35.9% is above both bull (5%) and sideways (25%) targets, but given: (1) TSLA must be sold at next rebalance, which will raise cash further before redeployment; (2) breadth narrowing to 57% with bearish 8MA trend reduces regime confidence and argues for sideways-level cash (25%); (3) portfolio state inconsistency between holdings and trade log means true invested amount is uncertain. Maintaining current cash level is appropriate until 2026-05-01 rebalance when full re-screening will establish clean positions. Do not add equity exposure before rebalance given TSLA concentration risk and earnings overhang.
runs.summary: Day-end review 2026-04-29: TSLA flagged SELL (volatility Risk Filter, quality deterioration, 5x concentration breach) for execution at 2026-05-01 rebalance open; AAPL flagged WATCH ahead of earnings tomorrow; regime downgraded to SIDEWAYS (confidence 58%) on narrowing breadth; portfolio state reconciliation required between holdings and same-day sell orders.

runs.assessment: Portfolio holds only 2 positions (AAPL and TSLA) entered on 2026-04-28, with critical data gaps — all fundamental and momentum metrics are zeroed out (eps_growth=0, momentum_rank=0, ma_50d=0), making rule-based sell decisions data-dependent. Cash is severely over-allocated at 59.1% against a bull regime target of 5%, and the full rebalance scheduled for 2026-05-01 (Thursday) is the priority action.
